description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Cleveland, Ohio, formed during the 1900s. Under boss John T. Scalish (1945-1976), it exerted influence over the Teamsters and skimmed Las Vegas casino revenue. Membership peaked at 60 made men in the 1950s. After a gang war in the late 1970s and subsequent convictions, the family weakened but remains active in illegal gambling and loansharking.

country_links.evidence_quote: The four Lonardo brothers ... and seven Porrello brothers ... were immigrants to the United States from Licata, Sicily, Italy.
country_links.context: The Lonardo and Porrello brothers immigrated from Licata, Sicily, Italy, and the organization's early members originated from there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1935, Milano fled to Mexico after being indicted for tax evasion.
country_links.context: Former boss Frank Milano fled to Mexico after being indicted for tax evasion.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Cleveland crime family, also known as the Scalish crime family or the Cleveland Mafia, is an Italian American Mafia crime family based in Cleveland, Ohio, and throughout the Greater Cleveland area.
country_links.context: The Cleveland crime family is based in the United States and operates there.
